object_types.name: Patient
object_types.definition: A person who receives medical care at the clinic, identified by a medical record number.

source_references.excerpt: A patient books an appointment with a doctor. We identify patients by their medical record number, which is assigned at registration.

object_types.name: Doctor
object_types.definition: A medical practitioner at the clinic, identified by a state-issued provider ID.

source_references.excerpt: Each doctor has a provider ID issued by the state medical board.

object_types.name: Appointment
object_types.definition: A scheduled visit between a patient and a doctor, identified by a confirmation number.

source_references.excerpt: Each appointment gets a unique confirmation number when it's created.

object_types.name: ExamRoom
object_types.definition: A physical examination room at the clinic, identified by a room number.

source_references.excerpt: Each exam room has a room number.

object_types.name: Specialty
object_types.definition: The medical specialty of a doctor.

source_references.excerpt: things like cardiology, dermatology, family medicine, orthopedics, or pediatrics

object_types.name: AppointmentDate
object_types.definition: The calendar date of an appointment.

source_references.excerpt: The date, a time slot, and a reason for the visit.

object_types.name: TimeSlot
object_types.definition: A half-hour time slot during clinic hours.

object_types.name: VisitReason
object_types.definition: Free-text reason for the appointment as described by the patient.

source_references.excerpt: The reason is free text -- whatever the patient describes when they call.

object_types.name: AppointmentStatus
object_types.definition: The current status of an appointment in its lifecycle.

source_references.excerpt: scheduled, checked-in, in-progress, completed, cancelled, or no-show

object_types.name: FollowUpNote
object_types.definition: Free-text follow-up note attached to a completed appointment.

source_references.excerpt: A completed appointment may also have a follow-up note, which is free text.
A patient books an appointment with a doctor.
A patient books an appointment with a doctor.
Every doctor has a specialty
Every appointment must have a date and a time slot.
Every appointment must have a date and a time slot.
a reason for the visit. The reason is free text
An appointment has a status
A completed appointment may also have a follow-up note
we do track a "primary doctor" for each patient. Every patient must have a primary doctor on file.

inferred_constraints.description: Every Appointment is booked by some Patient.

inferred_constraints.description: Each Appointment is with at most one Doctor.

inferred_constraints.description: Every Appointment is with some Doctor.

source_references.excerpt: A patient books an appointment with a doctor.

inferred_constraints.description: Each Doctor has exactly one Specialty.

source_references.excerpt: A doctor has exactly one specialty.
Every doctor has a specialty

inferred_constraints.description: Each Appointment is on at most one AppointmentDate.

source_references.excerpt: Every appointment must have a date

inferred_constraints.description: Every Appointment is on some AppointmentDate.

source_references.excerpt: Every appointment must have a date

inferred_constraints.description: Each Appointment is at at most one TimeSlot.

source_references.excerpt: Every appointment must have a date and a time slot.

inferred_constraints.description: Every Appointment is at some TimeSlot.

source_references.excerpt: Every appointment must have a date and a time slot.

inferred_constraints.description: Each Appointment has at most one AppointmentStatus.

Every appointment starts as scheduled.

inferred_constraints.description: Each Patient has at most one primary Doctor.

source_references.excerpt: we do track a "primary doctor" for each patient

inferred_constraints.description: Every Patient has some primary Doctor.

source_references.excerpt: Every patient must have a primary doctor on file.

ambiguities.description: The transcript describes a composite uniqueness constraint: a doctor can only see one patient per time slot on a given day, and a patient can only have one appointment per time slot on a given day. These external uniqueness constraints span multiple fact types and cannot be represented as simple internal uniqueness constraints.

source_references.excerpt: a patient can only have one appointment per time slot on a given day -- no double-booking from the patient side.
A doctor can only see one patient per time slot on a given day.

ambiguities.description: Room assignment is described as 'a doctor is assigned to a room for the day' which implies a ternary fact type involving Doctor, ExamRoom, and a date, with shared rooms across doctors on different days. This is not modeled as a simple binary.

source_references.excerpt: A doctor is assigned to a room for the day, but rooms can be shared across doctors on different days.
